data.title: Novo Nordisk gets FDA expanded approval for hemophilia treatment
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NVO) said it has received expanded approval from the FDA for Alhemo injection, as a treatment to help prevent or lessen bleeding episodes in both adult and pediatric patients aged 12 and older who have hemophilia A or B. 
  • This approval builds on the previous authorization granted in December 2024 for patients with hemophilia A or B who have inhibitors.
  • The approval is based on a late-stage trial data, which established the safety and efficacy of Alhemo

data.title: Jiangsu Easypark to acquire China Senior Living Industry International
data.description:
• Jiangsu Easypark Intelligent Technology announced on Friday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire China Senior Living Industry International  (OTC:CHYL).
  • Upon completion, Jiangsu Easypark intends to consolidate its core assets into CHYL, creating a strengthened entity well-positioned to capitalize on growing global demand for smart city infrastructure.
  • As part of the post-transaction integration plan, CHYL's board of directors will be restructured. Jin Weiming is expected to be appointed as chief executive officer, assuming full executive leadership of the combined company.
  • CHYL is -0.0% to $0.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Consumer sentiment final print shows slight improvement from June
data.description:
July Consumer Sentiment: 61.7 vs. the prior July estimate of 61.8 and 60.7 in June, according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.5% vs. prior print of +4.4% and +5.0% in June.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.4% vs. previous reading of +3.6% and 4.0% in June.

Current economic conditions index: 68.0 vs. 66.8 initial print and 64.8 in June.

Consumer expectations index was revised to 57.7 from the previous reading 58.6 and 58.1 in June.

data.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI contracts more than expected in July
data.description: The U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI  slid to 48.0 in June, vs. 49.5 consensus, from 49.0 in the prior month, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: AMZN, FIG, ROKU, MRNA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged down Friday morning as worries grew over the economic effects of President Trump's newly imposed higher tariffs ranging from 10% to 41%, affecting imports from over 90 countries, including key partners like Canada and India. Additionally, weak U.S. payroll data added to investor concerns about economic growth and monetary policy, contributing to the cautious market sentiment.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Figma (NYSE:FIG) +18% – Shares extended gains after a stunning NYSE debut on Thursday, where they soared 250% intraday, briefly topping $105. The design software firm priced its IPO at $33 per share, above the expected range, and saw its market value surpass $50B shortly after listing. The explosive rally marked one of the most dramatic IPO openings in recent memory.
  • Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) +14% – Shares rallied after the company delivered a double beat in Q2 and issued strong Q3 guidance. Daily active users rose 21% Y/Y to 110.4M, while revenue surged 78% to $500M, driven by 84% growth in ad sales. Q3 revenue is expected at $535M–$545M above the consensus of $472.7M with adjusted EBITDA of $185M–$195M (consensus $159.5M).
  • Kimberly-Clark (KMB) +7% – Shares rose despite Q2 earnings miss, as investors focused on the company’s highest volume growth in five years, which drove a 3.9% increase in organic sales even as total sales fell 1.6% Y/Y to $4.2B due to divestitures and FX headwinds. The company expects 2025 adjusted operating profit and EPS to grow at a low-to-mid single-digit rate on a constant-currency basis, though results will be weighed down by the PPE divestiture, U.S. private label diaper exit, and FX impacts, partly offset by a $0.16 per share tailwind from discontinued operations accounting.

Biggest stock losers

  • Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) -9% – Shares plunged after Q2 adjusted EPS of $0.12 fell far short of the $1.49 consensus, sharply down from $1.93 in the prior quarter and $1.10 a year ago. Total net revenue of $1.42B also missed estimates of $1.59B, declining from $1.96B in Q1 but up from $1.38B a year earlier. Looking ahead, Coinbase expects Q3 subscription and services revenue of $665M–$745M (midpoint $705M vs. $742M consensus), transaction expenses to grow in the mid-teens as a percentage of net revenue, tech and G&A costs of $800M–$850M, and sales and marketing spend of $190M–$290M.
  • Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) -7% – Shares declined even after the company topped Q2 expectations on revenue, EPS, and operating income, as its Q3 operating income guidance midpoint came in below consensus. Amazon expects Q3 revenue of $174.0B–$179.5B (midpoint $176.3B vs. $173.3B consensus) and operating income of $15.5B–$20.5B (midpoint $18.0B vs. $19.4B consensus). CEO Andy Jassy highlighted constraints in AI growth due to limited electricity and chip supply, noting capital expenditures of $31.4 billion in Q2 will likely persist for the remainder of the year as Amazon invests further in chips, data centers, and power infrastructure. 
  • Fluor (FLR) -29% – Shares plunged after the company missed Q2 estimates on both adjusted earnings and revenue and slashed its full-year guidance. Fluor now expects FY2025 adjusted EPS of $1.95–$2.15 (down from the prior view of $2.25–$2.75 and below the $2.58 consensus) and adjusted EBITDA of $475M–$525M (vs. the prior outlook of $575M–$675M), citing client hesitation amid economic uncertainty, delayed project awards, and continued pressure from legacy infrastructure projects. CEO Jim Breuer acknowledged short-term challenges but reaffirmed confidence in the company’s long-term strategy focused on disciplined execution and growth markets.
  • Roku (ROKU) -12% – Shares slumped despite better-than-expected Q2 results and the announcement of a $400M stock repurchase program, as investors focused on margin pressures. While Q3 revenue guidance of ~$1.2B (+13% Y/Y) topped the $1.17B consensus, and full-year revenue is expected to reach $4.65B (vs. $4.56B consensus), Q2 gross margins declined 0.9 pts Y/Y to 44.8%, driven by a 2.3 pt drop in platform margins to 51%. Roku raised its full-year 2025 outlook for platform revenue to $4.075B and adjusted EBITDA to $375M, citing strong free cash flow and improving profitability.
  • Moderna (MRNA) -7% – Shares slipped despite Q2 2025 results beating expectations, as the biotech trimmed the upper end of its full-year revenue forecast to $1.5B–$2.2B from a prior high of $2.5B. The cut was attributed to a timing shift in U.K. COVID vaccine shipments into Q1 2026. Moderna expects 40%-50% of H2 revenue to be recognized in Q3, with the rest in Q4, and reiterated its year-end cash and investments target of ~$6B.

data.title: Genius Sports deepens its bench in Europe with a new betting data contract
data.description:
Genius Sports Limited (NYSE:GENI) announced on Friday that it secured the exclusive official betting data rights for a select group of competitions within the European Leagues Association. As part of this multi-year partnership, Genius Sports (NYSE:GENI) will also deploy its GeniusIQ platform across European football.

The addition of European Leagues adds to the company's top-tier content portfolio that includes the Premier League and French Ligue 1. The European Leagues' members included in the agreement include theBelgian Pro League, the Ligová Fotbalová Asociace, the Danish League, the Dutch Eredivisie, the Dutch Keuken Kampioen Divisie, the Finnish Veikkausliiga, the Deutscher Fußball Bund, the Greek Super League, the Israeli Professional Football League, the Kazakhstan Football League, the Latvian Virsliga, the Norwegian Professional Football League, the Liga Portugal, the Romanian Professional Football League, tje Serbian Super Liga, the Slovak Niké Liga, the Föreningen Svensk Elitfotboll, and the Swiss Football League.

Shares of Genius Sports (GENI) opened with a 3.7% decline amid some broad market weakness. The sports data stock is still up more than 25% on a year-to-date basis and more than 60% over the last 52 weeks. Short interest on GENI stands at 12.4% of the total float.

data.title: Bayer gains after raising 2025 outlook
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) shares traded higher in Germany on Friday after the company lifted its full-year guidance for currency-adjusted sales and earnings, citing a better-than-expected performance from its pharma division.

Announcing its preliminary Q2 2025 results, the company revised its 2025 currency-adjusted financial outlook for revenue and core earnings per share to €46B–€48B and €4.80–€5.30 from the prior forecasts of €45B–€47B and €4.50–€5.00, respectively.

The German conglomerate also added an extra €1.2B ($1.37) provision related to its ongoing Roundup (glyphosate) litigation in the U.S., mainly to account for an adverse ruling on its appeal in the Anderson et al. case.

The additional provision came after a Missouri appellate court in May upheld a $611M verdict awarded to three people who alleged that Roundup caused their cancers.

As for preliminary Q2 results, Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) reported €1.23 of core earnings per share on group sales of ~€10.7, driven by 2.2% YoY growth in its Crop Science Division, while its Pharmaceuticals and Consumer Health divisions expanded 0.6% and 0.2%, respectively, on an adjusted basis.

data.title: U.S. Manufacturing PMI contracts less than expected in July
data.description: The S&P Global U.S. Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index fell to 49.8 in July from 52.9 in June, compared with the 49.5 consensus.

data.title: Cognex gets rating upgrade at KeyBanc after Q2 results, outlook
data.description:
KeyBanc Capital upgraded Cognex's (NASDAQ:CGNX) stock to Overweight from Sector Weight with a $50 price target.

Analysts led by Ken Newman said that following Cognex's second quarter 2025 results and subsequent earnings call, they upgraded the shares. Shares strongly outperformed after posting a modest second quarter beat and stronger-than-expected third quarter guidance ranges for both revenue and EBITDA.

Cognex's surged and closed about 20.76% on Thursday. However, the shares dipped about 2% on Friday.

The analysts said that specifically, the updated guidance implies an EBITDA flow-through margin of about 62% at the midpoint. The analysts think improving demand across most of the company's served end markets (i.e.. Warehouse/eCommerce, Packaging, and Factory Automation) could support a shift toward more normalized upcycle operating leverage into 2026 (with EBITDA incrementals averaging 55%+ since 2016).

Relative to KeyBanc's model, that implies out-year EBITDA margins in the low- to mid-20% range, well ahead of its 20% 2026 target introduced at its recent analyst day, and on track to meet its intermediate margin target of nearly over 25%, according to the analysts.

"While we acknowledge that macro visibility remains tepid, we think CGNX’s multiple likely already discounts that uncertainty, and think recently completed trade deals with Malaysia and Vietnam could allow some breathing room toward returning to more historical averages in the NT [near-term]," said Newman and his team.

data.title: Ares Management stock gaps down after Q2 earnings fall short of consensus
data.description:
Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) stock retreated 4.6% in Friday morning trading after the alternative investment manager second-quarter earnings that fell short of the average analyst estimate, despite strong gross fundings and market gains buoying assets under management.

Q2 adjusted GAAP EPS of $1.03, trailing the $1.09 average analyst estimate, slid from $1.09 in the prior quarter and rose from $0.99 in Q2 2024.

"Mixed contributions from fee-related earnings and realized performance income" drove the earnings miss, CFRA analyst Kenneth Leon wrote in a note to clients.

Even so, “we generated strong second quarter results, growing AUM, fee paying AUM and management fees by 24% or more year over year,” said CEO Michael Arougheti. “Our AUM benefited from our second highest level of quarterly gross fundraising coupled with record quarterly market appreciation driven by strong investment performance across our major strategies.

Fee-related earnings advanced to $397.8M in Q2 from $367.3M in Q1 and $324.5M in the year-ago period.

Realized income was $397.8M, down from $405.9M in Q1 and up from $363.2M a year before.

Total expenses of $1.14B compared with $1.01B in Q1 and $564.5M in Q2 2024.

“Our second quarter includes the first full quarter of financials from the GCP International acquisition, and we believe we are on track to meet our financial goals for this business, including greater than originally expected synergies that will be gradually realized over time,” said CFO Jarrod Phillips.

Gross new capital commitments totaled $26.2B in Q2, up from $20.2B in the prior three-month period. 

"We like that ARES has $151B of dry powder for future investment and $105B of AUM not yet paying fees within its funds," CFRA's Leon said.

Assets under management rose to $572.4B at June 30, 2025, from $545.9B at March 31, 2025, and climbed 28% from a year ago.

Conference call at 11:00 a.m. ET.
